briefing.title: Agent Economy Daily #11: NIST Makes It Official — AI Agents Get Their Own Standards Body
briefing.note: Practical write-up on Agent Economy Daily — more useful than the docs alone. Why it matters: Worth a side-by-side with what you're using now — Agent Economy Daily could change the tradeoffs.

briefing.title: Deep-Dive into LLM Fine-Tuning
briefing.note: Dive brings new tradeoffs to benchmark against what you're running. Why it matters: Dive moves the needle enough that existing model picks are worth re-checking.

editors_note.headline: This week
editors_note.thesis: This week's signal is clear: business model decisions are moving faster than product roadmaps. Trump Moves to Ban Anthropic From the US Government is the one to keep on your planning radar.
editors_note.hook: The headline drives the week, but the real question is what it means for your vendor assumptions and Q2 plans.
editors_note.spotlight: The lead story is the most likely to change near-term decisions for teams shipping AI products.
editors_note.underhyped: A Coding Implementation to Build a Hierarchical Planner AI Agent Using Open-Source LLMs with Tool Execution and Structured Multi-Agent Reasoning deserves more attention than it's getting.
editors_note.risk: Vendor and pricing risk is elevated this week — review your dependency list.

lead_story.title: Trump Moves to Ban Anthropic From the US Government
lead_story.what_happened: Developments around Trump are worth a slot in your next ops or leadership review.
lead_story.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input.
lead_story.what_to_do: Add Trump as a discussion item in your next strategy or planning meeting.

sections.title: Security

sections.promise: Security and compliance to act on.

articles.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
articles.summary: Security developments around Case may require patching or posture changes for affected teams (via Dark Reading).
articles.what_happened: Not urgent, but Case is the kind of thing that shows up again in a month.
articles.why_it_matters: Incidents involving Case put reliability and dependency risk back on the agenda for ops and leadership.
articles.what_to_do: Review Case and decide on one concrete follow-up action by end of week.
articles.our_take: Don't let FOMO drive architecture. Validate on your own terms before changing course.

sections.title: What changed in industry
sections.deck: Company moves, launches, and policy shifts that could affect your roadmap.
sections.promise: What changed and why it matters.

articles.title: Musk bashes OpenAI in deposition, saying ‘nobody committed suicide because of Grok’
articles.summary: If Musk pans out, it could change how teams approach their current stack (via TechCrunch AI).
articles.what_happened: Musk in the news; could affect pricing, roadmaps, or compliance this quarter — flag it.
articles.why_it_matters: Pricing or policy changes from Musk could affect your next renewal or roadmap; worth a slot in the sync.
articles.what_to_do: Flag Musk for your leadership team — it may shift near-term priorities.
articles.our_take: The signal is real; the timing matters more than the headline. Use it as a planning input (TechCrunch AI).

articles.title: Introducing the Stateful Runtime Environment for Agents in Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: A fresh release of Stateful Runtime Environment is now available for teams ready to evaluate alternatives (via OpenAI Blog).
articles.what_happened: A new release of Stateful Runtime Environment is now available, giving teams a fresh option to test.
articles.why_it_matters: With Stateful Runtime Environment now available, the options for your stack just expanded.
articles.what_to_do: Monitor Stateful Runtime Environment for follow-on product or pricing changes over the next month.
articles.our_take: The market is sending a signal. Teams that read it early get a head start.

sections.title: Tools to try
sections.deck: Tools worth testing in a real workflow this week.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Build a Hierarchical Planner AI Agent Using Open-Source LLMs with Tool Execution and Structured Multi-Agent Reasoning
articles.summary: The Coding Implementation paper offers techniques that applied teams should track for downstream impact (via MarkTechPost).
articles.what_happened: Coding Implementation released under an open license, giving teams full control over deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: The open release of Coding Implementation adds a viable alternative to proprietary options.
articles.what_to_do: Try Coding Implementation on a non-critical task this week and log the results.
articles.our_take: Don't adopt tools based on hype. Run it against your ugliest edge case first.

articles.title: Deploying Open Source Vision Language Models (VLM) on Jetson
articles.summary: Open access to VLM gives teams a new self-hosted option to evaluate (via Hugging Face Blog).
articles.what_happened: VLM going open-source lowers barriers for experimentation and custom deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: Open access to VLM lowers switching cost and pressures competing proprietary tools.
articles.what_to_do: Spin up VLM in a 2-hour proof of concept — compare output against your current tool.
articles.our_take: Ship a proof of concept before forming an opinion. The API surface matters more than the feature list.

prompt_of_the_week.text: Rewrite this API documentation to be developer-friendly. Keep it accurate but make it scannable with clear examples.
prompt_of_the_week.why: Bad docs are the #1 reason developers abandon tools. Asking for 'scannable with clear examples' targets the two things devs actually need: quick answers and copy-pasteable code.
prompt_of_the_week.tip: Paste the existing docs. Specify your audience (junior devs, senior engineers, etc.).
